item.title: Glycerol-driven TNAP activation in thermogenesis and mineralization
item.author: Mohammed Faiz HussainShreya S. KrishnanLawrence Kazak
item.content:encoded:
Glycerol is an allosteric activator of tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase (TNAP), activating the futile creatine cycle.

Mohammed Faiz HussainShreya S. KrishnanLawrence Kazak

Nature

item.title: Two-electron quantum walks for probing entanglement and decoherence in an electron microscope
item.author: Offek TzipermanDavid NabbenPeter Baum
item.content:encoded:
Entanglement between particles offers insights into quantum behaviour, but methods for studying it in free-electron systems are lacking. Now a two-electron quantum walk is used to probe decoherence of free electrons inside an electron microscope.

Offek TzipermanDavid NabbenPeter Baum

Nature Physics

item.title: Average topological phase in a disordered Rydberg atom array
item.author: Zongpei YueYu-Feng MaoLi You
item.content:encoded:
In addition to strongly protected topological phases that rely on exact symmetries, theory predicts that disorder can stabilize weakly protected phases in mixed quantum states, and an example of the latter is now observed in a Rydberg atom array.

Zongpei YueYu-Feng MaoLi You

Nature Physics

item.title: Inter-magnet pumping counters dissipation in artificial ferrimagnets
item.author: Kai ZhangY. X. NiuJ. Li
item.content:encoded:
Reducing dissipation when generating spin currents remains a central challenge in spintronics. Now, an artificial ferrimagnet is shown to produce spin current output and simultaneously lower magnetic damping.

Kai ZhangY. X. NiuJ. Li

Nature Physics

item.title: Daily briefing: Is a nine-to-five PhD possible?
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
PhD students share how they make their doctorate fit into the hours of a standard working week. Plus, male and female brain cells differ in gene activity and a whole new way to produce DNA.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Quantum computers take on health care: light-sensitive cancer drugs win US$2-million contest
item.author: Elizabeth Gibney
item.content:encoded:
Quantum machines are making inroads into biology but have no ‘advantage’ over classical machines yet.

Elizabeth Gibney

Nature

item.title: Efficient thermalization and universal quantum computing with quantum Gibbs samplers
item.author: Cambyse RouzéDaniel Stilck FrançaÁlvaro M. Alhambra
item.content:encoded:
Quantum simulation of equilibrium many-body systems requires the ability to sample from the thermal distribution of quantum states. An algorithm has now been proven to be an appropriate quantum analogue to classical Monte Carlo methods.

Cambyse RouzéDaniel Stilck FrançaÁlvaro M. Alhambra

Nature Physics

item.title: Intrinsic phononic dressed states in a nanomechanical system
item.author: M. YukselM. P. MaksymowychM. L. Roukes
item.content:encoded:
Exploiting the differences between quantum and classical physics typically requires nonlinear devices. Quantum nonlinear effects have now been demonstrated in a nanomechanical resonator due to strong coupling with an intrinsic two-level defect.

M. YukselM. P. MaksymowychM. L. Roukes

Nature Physics

item.title: NSF awards record number of coveted PhD fellowships in surprise move
item.author: Dan Garisto
item.content:encoded:
Quantum science and AI research are big winners just a year after the US funding giant slashed its Graduate Research Fellowship Program awards in half.

Dan Garisto

Nature

item.title: High-fidelity collisional quantum gates with fermionic atoms
item.author: Petar BojovićTimon HilkerTitus Franz
item.content:encoded:
A robust composite pair-exchange gate based on controlled interactions of fermionic atoms in an optical superlattice demonstrates high fidelities and long Bell-state lifetimes, marking an important step towards a fully digital fermionic quantum computer.

Petar BojovićTimon HilkerTitus Franz

Nature

item.title: Protected quantum gates using qubit doublons in dynamical optical lattices
item.author: Yann KieferZijie ZhuTilman Esslinger
item.content:encoded:
A purely geometric two-qubit SWAP gate can be realized by transiently populating qubit doublon states of fermionic atoms in a dynamical optical lattice.

Yann KieferZijie ZhuTilman Esslinger

Nature

item.title: Microcombs for the generation of tunable structured microwaves
item.author: Yuanhua FengXuebing Zhang
item.content:encoded:
Structured microwaves emitted by a chip-based microcomb source equipped with an antenna array enable ultrahigh-capacity communications and multi-variable sensing.

Yuanhua FengXuebing Zhang

Nature Photonics

item.title: High-precision measurement of the W boson mass with the CMS experiment
item.author: V. ChekhovskyA. HayrapetyanD. Druzhkin
item.content:encoded:
The CMS experiment at CERN reports one of the highest-precision measurements of the W boson mass, finding it in line with standard model predictions and at odds with recent anomalous measurements.

V. ChekhovskyA. HayrapetyanD. Druzhkin

Nature

item.title: Quantum ground-state cooling of two librational modes of a nanorotor
item.author: Stephan TroyerFlorian FechtelMarkus Arndt
item.content:encoded:
Controlling the rotational motion of nanoscale objects by trapping and cooling is a prerequisite for exploring quantum rotational phenomena. Now, two orthogonal librational modes of a levitated nanorotor are cooled into their quantum ground state.

Stephan TroyerFlorian FechtelMarkus Arndt

Nature Physics

item.title: All-optical polarization control in time-varying low-index films via plasma symmetry breaking
item.author: Wallace JaffraySven StengelMarcello Ferrera
item.content:encoded:
Femtosecond pumping of an isotropic low-index subwavelength film enables ultrafast polarization control, with induced birefringence up to 0.1π μm−1, a dichroic ratio of ~23% and a non-reciprocal optical activity of ±1.1° μm−1.

Wallace JaffraySven StengelMarcello Ferrera

Nature Photonics

item.title: Evidence for odd-parity superconductivity underpinned by antiferromagnetism in heavy-fermion metal YbRh2Si2
item.author: Lev V. LevitinJan KnappJohn Saunders
item.content:encoded:
Odd-parity spin-triplet superconductivity remains difficult to establish experimentally. Now, several distinct magnetic-field-tuned superconducting states—including a possible topological helical phase—have been identified in YbRh2Si2.

Lev V. LevitinJan KnappJohn Saunders

Nature Physics

item.title: Massive budget cuts for US science proposed again by Trump administration
item.author: Max KozlovDan GaristoEdward Chen
item.content:encoded:
The 2027 budget proposal would curb federal payments for scientific publishing and reduce funding for many US institutions.

Max KozlovDan GaristoEdward Chen

Nature

item.title: Noise-induced shallow circuits and the absence of barren plateaus
item.author: Antonio Anna MeleArmando AngrisaniYihui Quek
item.content:encoded:
Calculations on near-term quantum computers will be limited by the effects of noise. It has now been shown how different kinds of noise limit the achievable computational advantage of many proposed quantum algorithms.

Antonio Anna MeleArmando AngrisaniYihui Quek

Nature Physics

item.title: Low-overhead fault-tolerant quantum computation by gauging logical operators
item.author: Dominic J. WilliamsonTheodore J. Yoder
item.content:encoded:
Combining quantum error correction with gauge theory concepts from many-body physics enables the design of codes with improved resource requirements for fault-tolerant quantum computation.

Dominic J. WilliamsonTheodore J. Yoder

Nature Physics

item.title: A molecular probe for quantum metrology
item.author: Cun-Feng ChengShui-Ming Hu
item.content:encoded:
Cavity-enhanced spectroscopy has now reached temperatures as low as 4 K — colder than most of space. This removes long-standing barriers in measuring hydrogen, which is a benchmark system for testing quantum theory and relevant for metrology.

Cun-Feng ChengShui-Ming Hu

Nature Physics

item.title: ‘It’s a real shock’: quantum-computing breakthroughs pose imminent risks to cybersecurity
item.author: Davide Castelvecchi
item.content:encoded:
Two analyses suggest that quantum computers could crack ubiquitous security keys and cryptocurrencies before the decade is over.

Davide Castelvecchi

Nature

item.title: Local and remote synthesis of single-photon space-time wave packets
item.author: Bryan L. TuroBahaa E. A. SalehAyman F. Abouraddy
item.content:encoded:
By projecting the multimoded, spatiotemporally structured signal photon onto a single spatial mode to herald the arrival of the idler photon, a diffraction-free single-photon space-time wave packet in the idler that did not traverse the spectral modulator is remotely prepared.

Bryan L. TuroBahaa E. A. SalehAyman F. Abouraddy

Nature Photonics

item.title: Daily briefing: Quantum computers could crack cybersecurity systems before 2030
item.author: Jacob Smith
item.content:encoded:
Quantum-computing advances could pose an imminent threat to systems such as Internet communications. Plus, the progress of the Artemis II Moon mission and a high-speed plane chase for climate research.

Jacob Smith

Nature

item.title: Entanglement and electronic coherence in attosecond molecular photoionization
item.author: L.-M. KollA. J. Suñer-RubioM. J. J. Vrakking
item.content:encoded:
Dissociative ionization of H2 molecules by the combination of a phase-locked attosecond laser pair and a few-cycle NIR laser shows that ion–photoelectron entanglement influences electronic coherence in H2+, allowing control over the degree of entanglement by varying the delay between the pulses.

L.-M. KollA. J. Suñer-RubioM. J. J. Vrakking

Nature

item.title: Daily briefing: The countdown to NASA’s Artemis II Moon mission launch
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
The science that NASA’s latest Moon mission will conduct, and why some researchers aren’t fussed about it. Plus, the status of the bird flu outbreak in US cows and what publishers can do about hallucinated citations in scientific literature.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Dual-symmetry-guided assembly of complex lattices
item.author: Huang FangXiaotian LiPeng Tan
item.content:encoded:
A dual-symmetry-guided strategy is used to assemble a broad class of complex Archimedean lattices and two-dimensional quasicrystalline structures, providing a general and experimentally accessible route to complex-symmetry materials.

Huang FangXiaotian LiPeng Tan

Nature

item.title: Daily briefing: Are boys really in crisis? What the science says
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
Concerns about boys are widespread, but evidence shows that worries are best understood in the context of all young people. Plus, the month’s best science images and a whole new way that vertebrates slurp.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Observation of an obstructed atomic band in a transition metal dichalcogenide
item.author: Dumitru CălugăruYi JiangMiguel M. Ugeda
item.content:encoded:
Atomic insulators come in two varieties: so-called unobstructed and obstructed types. The former are common and now scanning tunnelling microscopy experiments provide evidence for the latter.

Dumitru CălugăruYi JiangMiguel M. Ugeda

Nature Physics

item.title: Humanity is heading back to the Moon — why aren’t more scientists thrilled?
item.author: Elizabeth GibneyDavide Castelvecchi
item.content:encoded:
Some researchers remain underwhelmed by NASA’s upcoming lunar fly-by.

Elizabeth GibneyDavide Castelvecchi

Nature

item.title: Observation of giant nonlinear valley Hall effect
item.author: Pan HeMin ZhangJian Shen
item.content:encoded:
Previous observations of the valley Hall effect have been limited to the linear regime. Now a nonlinear version is demonstrated with a larger magnitude than in the linear case.

Pan HeMin ZhangJian Shen

Nature Physics

item.title: Quantum simulations verified by experiments for the first time
item.author: Davide Castelvecchi
item.content:encoded:
Physicists cross-checked quantum computer predictions against experimental data about materials’ properties.

Davide Castelvecchi

Nature

item.title: Anthony Leggett obituary: physicist who brought quantum theory to the macro world
item.author: Philip Stamp
item.content:encoded:
The polymath also trained as a philosopher and won a Nobel prize for his theory of superfluids.

Philip Stamp

Nature

item.title: Large exciton longitudinal–transverse splitting enables room-temperature ultrafast coherent polaritonics in layered perovskite crystals
item.author: Rui CaiSiow Mean LohTze Chien Sum
item.content:encoded:
The researchers demonstrate room-temperature control over bulk exciton–polaritons (EPs) in a bare halide perovskite crystal and show that long-range bulk EP propagation occurs in the ballistic regime. Furthermore, slow light is demonstrated across the visible wavelength range.

Rui CaiSiow Mean LohTze Chien Sum

Nature Photonics

item.title: Trump’s new science advisers include 12 technology chiefs — and one academic
item.author: Dan Garisto
item.content:encoded:
Sparse academic presence on the US President’s Council of Advisors on Science and Technology reflects a focus on technology and industry.

Dan Garisto

Nature

item.title: Long-distance quantum link generates entanglement faster than it is lost
item.author: Ronald HansonTracy Northup
item.content:encoded:
Entanglement between ions connected by 10 kilometres of optical fibre is a step towards large-scale quantum communications networks.

Ronald HansonTracy Northup

Nature

item.title: Superluminal correlations in ensembles of optical phase singularities
item.author: T. BucherA. GorlachI. Kaminer
item.content:encoded:
Ultrafast electron imaging shows full phase-space dynamics of optical singularities, which can reach superluminal velocities before annihilation and break the particle-like analogy of topological defects.

T. BucherA. GorlachI. Kaminer

Nature

item.title: Drowning in data sets? Here’s how to cut them down to size
item.author: Sarah Wild
item.content:encoded:
Indefinite data retention is neither financially nor practically possible, but there are ways to give your data maximal long-term value.

Sarah Wild

Nature

item.title: Orders-of-magnitude improvement in precision spectroscopy of an inner-shell orbital clock transition in neutral ytterbium
item.author: Taiki IshiyamaKoki OnoYoshiro Takahashi
item.content:encoded:
Precision spectroscopy of an inner-shell orbital clock transition in Yb is developed. It reveals the transition moment, the intrinsic lifetime of the excited state and the interorbital Feshbach resonance. Isotope shifts with uncertainties below 10 Hz are also measured.

Taiki IshiyamaKoki OnoYoshiro Takahashi

Nature Photonics

item.title: Exceptional sensitivity near the bistable transition point of a hybrid quantum system
item.author: Hanfeng WangKurt JacobsMatthew E. Trusheim
item.content:encoded:
Operating devices close to a phase transition can improve performance due to the singular behaviour at critical points. An enhancement in sensitivity has now been achieved using the bistable transition point in a hybrid quantum system.

Hanfeng WangKurt JacobsMatthew E. Trusheim

Nature Physics

item.title: Elusive ‘nuclear clocks’ tick closer to reality — after decades in the making
item.author: Dan Garisto
item.content:encoded:
Super-precise timekeepers based on atomic nuclei could be tested as soon as this year.

Dan Garisto

Nature

item.title: Daily briefing: Why stress causes eczema to flare up in mice
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
A group of stress-responsive skin neurons produce inflammatory proteins that drive eczema symptoms. Plus, the ongoing debate about how people first settled in South America and how people are greening their traditional tent homes in Mongolia.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: UK bets big on homegrown fusion and quantum — can it lead the world?
item.author: David Adam
item.content:encoded:
UK government announces multibillion-pound science investments — but what impact will this have on the global race in these fields?

David Adam

Nature

item.title: Major Turing computing award goes to quantum science for first time
item.author: Davide Castelvecchi
item.content:encoded:
Turing Award winners Gilles Brassard and Charles Bennett pioneered ideas that are now foundational to quantum computers and quantum communications.

Davide Castelvecchi

Nature

item.title: Observation of self-bound droplets of ultracold dipolar molecules
item.author: Siwei ZhangWeijun YuanSebastian Will
item.content:encoded:
Self-bound droplets and droplet arrays are observed in an ultracold gas of strongly dipolar sodium–caesium molecules, establishing ultracold molecules as a system for the exploration of strongly dipolar quantum matter.

Siwei ZhangWeijun YuanSebastian Will

Nature

item.title: The E3 ubiquitin ligase mechanism specifying targeted microRNA degradation
item.author: Jakob FarnungElena SlobodyanyukDavid P. Bartel
item.content:encoded:
Target-directed microRNA degradation is driven by the atypical ZSWIM8–CUL3 E3 ubiquitin ligase that uses a two-RNA-factor authentication mechanism to specifically recognize AGO–miRNA–trigger RNA complexes and polyubiquitylate AGO.

Jakob FarnungElena SlobodyanyukDavid P. Bartel

Nature

item.title: Daily briefing: Funding calls plummet as NIH turns away from agency-directed science
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
The US National Institutes of Health will now focus on ‘unsolicited’ research proposals driven by the interests of individual scientists. Plus, the oldest known recording of a whale’s song and how tiny electric vehicles can help to change the world.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Experimental memory control in continuous-variable optical quantum reservoir computing
item.author: Iris PaparelleJohan HenaffValentina Parigi
item.content:encoded:
Researchers demonstrate a photonic quantum reservoir computing platform that uses spectral and temporal multiplexing in a continuous-variable setting. Real-time memory is implemented, and nonlinear temporal tasks are enabled.

Iris PaparelleJohan HenaffValentina Parigi

Nature Photonics

item.title: Origin of strange metallicity in a d-orbital kagome metal
item.author: Jean C. SouzaMoshe HaimHaim Beidenkopf
item.content:encoded:
The mechanism of strange metallicity remains difficult to understand. Now it is shown that in a strongly correlated d-orbital kagome metal, compact orbitals created by destructive interference can produce the unusual electronic behaviour.

Jean C. SouzaMoshe HaimHaim Beidenkopf

Nature Physics

item.title: Bose–Hubbard simulator with long-range hopping
item.author: Camille LagoinCorentin MorinFrançois Dubin
item.content:encoded:
Simulating the Bose–Hubbard model with physical systems is an important fundamental task. Now it is shown that dipolar excitons emulate a version of this model in which bosons can hop beyond their nearest neighbours.

Camille LagoinCorentin MorinFrançois Dubin

Nature Physics

item.title: A bucket-brigade quantum random access memory
item.author: Fanhao ShenYujie JiJianwei Yin
item.content:encoded:
Random access memory has multiple data registers and uses addresses to specify which register should be read or modified. Now a quantum random access memory has been demonstrated that uses quantum addresses to return data in superposition.

Fanhao ShenYujie JiJianwei Yin

Nature Physics
